mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate wage income and consumption, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending forecasts and triggers risk-off repositioning in equities.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE has risen >0.3pp over the prior 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and decrease in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a monetary tightening that is constraining real economic output, amplifying downside equity risk beyond either factor alone.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS changes and INDPRO changes does not add significant explanatory power (incremental R-squared < 0.01) for 60-day forward returns in a pooled regression.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds 1.5%, monetary policy is in an active tightening cycle that raises discount rates applied to equity cash flows and compresses valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.5%, positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 55% of months, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in real retail and food services sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand that translates into immediate revenue surprises for consumer-facing sectors, generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following month-over-month RSAFS growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) indicates improving manufacturing efficiency and capacity utilization, supporting corporate margin expansion and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) of more than 10% over a 6-month period signals weakening construction activity and downstream demand for durable goods, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the bad news is priced in.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when HOUST has declined >10% over the prior 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 0.5% signals a negative supply shock that compresses corporate margins and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO contracts by >0.5% month-over-month.
